topics.title: Satellite Radio Growth and Jon Stewart Critique
topics.summary: The studio discusses the success of satellite radio reach, the impact of celebrity mentions on search engine trends, and a critique of Jon Stewart's television ratings versus his critical acclaim.

topics.title: Media Commentary and Cultural Observations
topics.summary: The show features a discussion on the risks of news reporting in Iraq, the distinction between comedians and news sources, an analysis of a controversial religious sermon, and brief commentary on local weather and music.

topics.title: Oprah Winfrey Endorses Fraudulent Memoir
topics.summary: The studio discusses a lawsuit against author James Fry for publishing a fictionalized memoir that received an endorsement from Oprah Winfrey.

topics.title: Class Action Lawsuit Over Book Content
topics.summary: The show discusses a class-action lawsuit filed by readers seeking reimbursement for their time spent reading a book that was criticized for lack of fact-checking.

topics.title: Homocop Comedy Sketch and Radio News
topics.summary: The studio features a comedic skit about a robot cop named Homocop, followed by discussions regarding satellite radio expansion, guest appearances, and upcoming comedy segments.

topics.title: NFL Super Bowl Predictions and Player News
topics.summary: The studio features an NFL player who discusses his predictions for the upcoming Super Bowl, the defensive strengths of the Pittsburgh team, and the recent legal troubles involving teammate Chris Henry.

topics.title: Chad Johnson Interview and Commercial Promotion
topics.summary: The studio features an interview with Cincinnati Bengals player Chad Johnson, who discusses his contract, personal preferences, and a new commercial for Degree deodorant.

topics.title: Pat Monahan and Relationship Discussion
topics.summary: The studio features a conversation with Pat Monahan of the band Train regarding his recent divorce, his new relationship, and an anecdote about a previous recording session.

topics.title: Pat Monahan and Train Performance
topics.summary: The studio features a live performance of "Drops of Jupiter" by Pat Monahan of the band Train, followed by a discussion regarding his relationship history, family life, and the inspiration behind his music.

topics.title: Train Band Performance and Guest Interviews
topics.summary: The studio hosts Pat Monahan of the band Train and his girlfriend Amber to discuss their new album, perform a cover of "Dream On," and engage in personal anecdotes with callers.

topics.title: Jay Thomas's Career Transition from Radio to Television
topics.summary: The studio explores Jay Thomas's transition from a successful radio personality in the 1970s to an actor on television shows like Mork & Mindy and Cheers, including his experiences working alongside Robin Williams.

topics.title: Jay Thomas Interview
topics.summary: The studio hosts Jay Thomas and discuss his career history, personal anecdotes involving Robin Williams, and his current role on Sirius Satellite Radio.

topics.title: Coretta Scott King Death Announcement
topics.summary: The show concludes with the announcement of the death of Coretta Scott King, the widow of civil rights leader Martin Luther King.

topics.title: Death of Coretta Scott King and Industry Tributes
topics.summary: The studio discusses the passing of Coretta Scott King, pays tribute to several deceased adult film stars, reviews Oscar nominations, and reflects on Dave Chappelle's career.

topics.title: Awards Season and Current Events
topics.summary: The studio discusses upcoming Oscar nominees, the State of the Union address, reports on Bob Woodruff, and various celebrity news items.

topics.title: Train Band Performance and Interview
topics.summary: The studio hosts a discussion with members of the band Train regarding their new single "Cab" and their upcoming tour schedule.
